seoTitle: How to Add Form 1098-T to TurboTax Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to locate and enter your 1098-T tuition form in TurboTax to claim education tax credits and avoid common filing mistakes.
intro: The 1098-T form reports tuition payments that can unlock valuable education tax credits, but only if it's entered correctly in TurboTax. This guide walks through finding your form, entering it step by step, and avoiding mistakes that could cost you savings. It's aimed at students, parents, and tax professionals who want to make sure education expenses are captured accurately.

steps.heading: Locate your 1098-T form
steps.body: Your school typically issues the 1098-T form, so start by retrieving it from your student portal or the mail before entering anything into TurboTax.

steps.heading: Enter the information in TurboTax
steps.body: Within the education section of TurboTax, follow the prompts to input the figures from your 1098-T, including tuition paid and any scholarships received.

steps.heading: Review credits you may qualify for
steps.body: Correctly entering your 1098-T helps TurboTax determine whether you qualify for education-related tax credits based on your reported expenses.

steps.heading: Avoid common filing mistakes
steps.body: Double-check that scholarship amounts and tuition figures match what's on your actual form, since mismatched numbers can reduce your eligible credit.

steps.heading: Use best practices for future filings
steps.body: Keeping your 1098-T and related receipts organized each year makes it easier to enter accurate information and maximize savings going forward.

faq.question: Who receives a 1098-T form?
faq.answer: Students who paid qualifying tuition and related expenses at an eligible institution typically receive a 1098-T from their school.

faq.question: Can parents claim the 1098-T on their own return?
faq.answer: In many cases a parent can claim education credits based on a dependent student's 1098-T, though it depends on the specific filing situation.

faq.question: What if I never received my 1098-T form?
faq.answer: If your form is missing, check your student portal first, since many schools make it available electronically even if a paper copy wasn't mailed.

faq.question: Does entering a 1098-T guarantee a tax credit?
faq.answer: Not automatically; TurboTax uses the figures you enter to determine if you qualify for a credit based on income and other eligibility rules.

faq.question: Are scholarships reported on the 1098-T taxable?
faq.answer: Scholarship amounts can be taxable in certain situations, particularly if they exceed qualified education expenses, so it's worth reviewing that section carefully.
